title: Lowering
description: An authored realization binding from one upper-dialect record to one or more lower-dialect records, per design/lowering-record-sketch-01.md L1-L10. The load-bearing cross-boundary record — every *_lowerings.py encoding module authors these. Realization coupling only (L1 guardrail); Characterization / Claim-moment / Claim-trajectory couplings live on the verifier surface per verification-sketch-01 V3 and ship under schema/verification/. The record carries a CrossDialectRef upper_record, a tuple of CrossDialectRef lower_records, an Annotation with prose rationale, a status (active/pending per L8), and optional position_range + anchor_τ_a + metadata. Inline $defs for the three id-less sub-records per PFS7-X2. PFS9-L1..L10.

cross_dialect_ref.description: A typed reference to a record in a specific dialect. Used by Lowering.upper_record and each element of Lowering.lower_records; the same shape also lives in the verification namespace (re-inlined there per PFS8-V guidance). Plain-string dialect token per PFS9-X4 (open — no closed enum at the schema layer; architecture-sketch-02 A6 admits author-defined dialects). PFS9-X1.

dialect.description: Dialect token (e.g., 'substrate', 'dramatic', 'dramatica_complete', 'aristotelian', 'save_the_cat'). Open string per PFS9-X4; typo-detection is a verifier-runtime concern. Extension path is zero-cost: a new dialect uses its own token.

record_id.description: Stable identifier for the referenced record in its dialect (e.g., 'C_oedipus' under dramatic, 'E_oedipus_anagnorisis' under substrate). Resolution is the dialect's own concern — this record just carries the pair.

annotation.description: Prose rationale for the Lowering binding, per L5. The annotation is where the author's reasoning lives — why these lower records realize this upper record. review_states carries reviewer verdicts (via cross-file $ref to annotation_review.json per PFS9-X2). PFS9-X1.

text.description: Free-form prose. Empty string admitted (an author may commit a Lowering with a stub annotation and fill in the rationale later).

attention.description: Closed enum matching Python's ATTENTION_* constants (mirrored from descriptions-sketch-01's attention vocabulary). structural = most Lowerings (assertions about realization); interpretive = discussion of how well the binding reads; flavor = author-side commentary not expected to be pressed. Closure commits the three-value vocabulary at the schema layer; reversal path via PFS9 OQ4 if a fourth value ever surfaces.

review_states.description: Optional array of AnnotationReview objects via cross-file $ref to annotation_review.json (PFS9-X2). Empty array legal; Python default empty tuple.

position_range.description: Positional correspondence when both upper and lower dialects use ordering coordinates and the Lowering covers a range. Per L7. Inclusive integer bounds. PFS9-X1.

coord.description: Lower-dialect coordinate name (e.g., 'τ_d' for substrate sjuzhet ordering, 'narrative_position' for Dramatic scene order).

id.description: Stable identifier for this Lowering (e.g., 'L_oedipus_anagnorisis', 'L_macbeth_ambition'). No prefix convention enforced; encodings use 'L_' descriptively. PFS9-L2.

upper_record.description: The single upper-dialect record this Lowering realizes, as a CrossDialectRef. Per L2 — one upper per Lowering; many-to-many at the collection level (multiple Lowerings can share an upper_record). PFS9-L3.

lower_records.description: Array of lower-dialect records that realize the upper. Per L3 + L8 — empty array is admitted ONLY when status is 'pending' (see allOf clause below); active Lowerings require at least one lower record. PFS9-L4.

annotation.description: Prose rationale for the binding per L5. See $defs/annotation. PFS9-L5.

authored_by.description: Optional provenance string. Python default 'author'; dump emits unconditionally. PFS9-L6.

τ_a.description: The Lowering's own authored τ_a, distinct from anchor_τ_a (which snapshots the lower side's τ_a at author time for staleness). Python default 0; dump emits unconditionally. PFS9-L7.

status.description: Closed enum matching Python's LoweringStatus. ACTIVE is the default (dump always emits); PENDING admits Lowerings authored against upper records whose lower realization doesn't yet exist (promissory bindings — lowering-sketch-01 F5's gap findings are authored as PENDING). PFS9-L8.

position_range.description: Optional positional correspondence per L7 (see $defs/position_range). Omitted from dump when None in Python. PFS9-L9.

anchor_τ_a.description: Optional snapshot of the lower side's τ_a at author time. Per L6 — used for staleness detection (if a lower record's current τ_a exceeds this snapshot, the Lowering is stale). Python None → omitted from dump. Character → Entity Lowerings typically have anchor_τ_a=None because Entities don't carry τ_a (per oedipus_lowerings.py); anticipated non-finding 3 of PFS9. PFS9-L10.

metadata.description: Optional open-object metadata. Admits any keys per PFS8 OQ1's posture (matches substrate-sketch-05's metadata-as-string-dict convention). Known keys today: 'supersedes' (points at a superseded Lowering id), 'superseded_by' (back-reference). Dump emits only when non-empty; validate_lowerings carries referential-integrity checks for known keys (supersedes_unresolved, supersession_back_reference_missing codes). Per-key schemas deferred to PFS9 OQ1.

allOf.description: L8 conditional per PFS9-X3: status='active' requires at least one lower_record. PENDING Lowerings admit empty lower_records (promissory bindings). Parallel to PFS5-B5 (Branch kind conditional) and PFS6-M6 (plot_kind=complex conditional). The Python __post_init__ raises ValueError on the schema-violating case; this clause encodes the same constraint at the schema layer.
